steps: By homotopy invariance, $H_*(X) \cong H_*(\{*\})$ for any contractible $X$.
For a point $\{*\}$, the singular chain complex has $C_0 = \mathbb{Z}$ (the unique 0-simplex) and $C_k = \mathbb{Z}$ for all $k$, but all boundary maps alternate between $0$ and $\text{id}$.
In fact the augmented complex $\cdots \to C_1 \to C_0 \to \mathbb{Z} \to 0$ is acyclic, giving $H_0 = \mathbb{Z}$ and $H_k = 0$ for $k \geq 1$.
For $D^n$: the radial deformation $H(x,t) = (1-t)x$ contracts $D^n$ to the origin, so $D^n$ is contractible and $H_k(D^n) = 0$ for $k \geq 1$.
